name: separator
description: Visual or semantic divider between content

meta.name: Separator
meta.description: Visual or semantic divider between content

props.name: decorative
props.description: When true, the separator is purely visual and hidden from assistive tech

whenToUse: Dividing groups of content within a section or menu
Adding a vertical rule between inline items (orientation="vertical")
whenNotToUse: Adding spacing only — use margin/padding, not a separator
Bordering a container — use the container border, not a separator

antiPatterns.bad: Multiple separators stacked to create whitespace
antiPatterns.good: Use spacing tokens for gaps; one separator for a real break
antiPatterns.why: Separators imply a content division; using them for spacing misleads assistive tech

related.name: Card
related.reason: Often used to divide regions inside a Card

intent.a11yRationale: role="separator" when meaningful so screen readers announce the division; setting decorative hides it from the accessibility tree when it is purely visual

flexibility.note: Mark decorative when the break carries no meaning beyond visuals
